items.title: Growing frustration in real estate industry over Qld seller disclosure laws

children.content: Real estate experts say Queensland's seller disclosure laws, which were introduced in August, are fuelling 'gazumping' and leading to unnecessary costs.

items.title: Man charged over Queensland hate speech laws faces court

children.content: Liam Parry, 33, was arrested and charged last month for reciting the banned phrase "from the river to the sea."

image.altText: A man speaks in front of several microphones

items.title: Water storage feud heats up as frustrated farmers take on corporate giants

children.content: Central Queensland irrigators are demanding a "fair go" from two mega water users that are pumping from a ratepayer-owned storage without paying anything for its multimillion-dollar costs.

items.title: Cost of filling best-selling car up by $100 since start of Iran war

children.content: The ABC analyses how the cost of a full tank in Australia's most-purchased cars of 2025 has changed since the beginning of the Iran war.

image.altText: A row of ford utes outside a dealership.

items.title: Qld premier says federal government should fast-track oil projects

children.content: Queensland Premier David Crisafulli wants the federal government to fast-track environmental approvals for oil projects.

image.altText: Aerial photo looking down a street in Taroom with storm clouds in the distance, Queensland November 2021.

items.title: Smallest town in Australia for sale as two residents decide to sell roadhouse

children.content: Cooladdi, nine hours west of Brisbane, dwindled in population when its railway station closed, but it continues to attract road-trippers and graziers. Its owners run local services including a shop, motel and mail service.

items.title: Claims wild-caught Australian seafood will soon 'only be afforded by the rich'

children.content: Australian seafood prices are likely to rise due to fuel supply issues, with some warning that wild-caught seafood could become unaffordable for most.

image.altText: Prawns at a seafood market

items.title: Tourists urged not to cancel plans as Cyclone Maila intensifies

children.content: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila is now a category five system and is expected to cross into Far North Queensland on Sunday.

items.title: Husband's dying wish for more kids an impossible choice in childcare desert

items.title: Tourism industry council warns sector at 'tipping point'

children.content: According to the Queensland Tourism Industry Council (QTIC), many operators are struggling to remain afloat as they grapple with rising operating costs and cancellations, along with the ongoing fuel crisis.

children.content: The latest incident comes a day after a car was allegedly rammed into a Gold Coast car wash business and set alight.

items.title: Townsville victim counts cost of crime spree that saw nine arrested

children.content: Daniel Toppi was just starting to get back on his feet before a group of teenagers allegedly stole his work vehicle. Police have arrested nine teenagers in relation to the incident that also included the alleged theft of a police vehicle.

items.title: Man charged over alleged carjacking spree across southern Queensland

items.title: Broncos name Walsh replacement, Rooster returns from 10-match suspension

items.title: Police allege P-plater caught driving 54km over limit on beach at Easter

children.content: Police crack down on beach driving these school holidays — alleging two minors were driving unlicensed and a P-plater was driving 54km/h over the speed limit in a 40km/h zone.

items.title: Pheobe Bishop murder-accused to face court on anniversary of 17yo's disappearance

children.content: The case of a woman accused of murdering Queensland teenager Pheobe Bishop has been adjourned to May 15 — the first anniversary of Pheobe's disappearance.

items.title: No children faced court on new 'adult crime, adult time' offences in past 11 years

children.content: Twelve new offences are set to be added to the government's adult crime, adult time legislation, including offences that no children have faced court on in the past 11 years.

items.title: A luxury beef giant's cattle died of thirst. Regulators were told far later

children.content: The lack of investigation into deaths at the $700 million beef business has been described as "completely unacceptable" by a legal expert who argued industry had captured government regulators.

items.title: Farmers push for stronger protections in renewable energy policy

children.content: Farmers in north Queensland want a say in how renewable energy companies negotiate with councils, amid concerns poorly designed agreements could have long-term impacts on agricultural land and rural communities.

items.title: Wrecked boats spill styrofoam, chemicals into Great Barrier Reef for months

children.content: The vessel 7 Wonder has sat wrecked at Rose Bay in north Queensland for more than two months. Locals say they have been picking up rubbish from the boat daily, but maritime authorities say local salvage contractors are "stretched".

items.title: A great gift or unexpected curse? Reflections on life as a new retiree

children.content: While time is retirement's great gift, it can become a curse for anyone who has devoted decades to a career and then suddenly wakes up with relevance deprivation syndrome and no firm plan on what comes next, writes Matt Eaton.
